narrative.headline: What 2017 Ford Escape owners report most
narrative.summary: Engine stalling is the dominant pattern in owner complaints for this model year, with reports of the engine cutting out at low speeds and in reverse. A secondary cluster of complaints involves the powertrain producing grinding sounds during tight turns. Electrical system issues, including heating and air conditioning malfunctions accompanied by a burning smell, round out the reported problems.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the engine stalling at low speeds, including while reversing out of a driveway, with the vehicle moving only a short distance before cutting out. · 10 fire · 7 crash · 5 injured

issues.title: Engine And Engine Cooling
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle sputtering and stalling at various speeds, accompanied by the check engine light illuminating, with a faulty EVAP system noted as part of the complaint. · 1 crash

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report a loud grinding sound coming from the rear of the vehicle when making tight turns, a noise not present in other comparable vehicles. · 3 fire · 5 crash · 5 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report heating and air conditioning problems, with at least one complaint describing detection of a burning smell after extended highway driving. · 1 fire · 2 crash · 4 injured
